Content: Rice: Last week was, I think, the Kickstarter update went out.
Content_zh: Rice：上星期應該是 Kickstarter 更新發出去了。

Content: So that went over really well and there's a lot of comments about how we all looked really sad in that episode.
Content_zh: 反應很好，而且很多留言都說我們在那集看起來很難過。

Content: Schafer: Cheer up, you guys!
Content_zh: Schafer：振作點！

Content: Petty: There were a couple random cuts of me going; that had nothing to do with what was being said.
Content_zh: Petty：有幾個隨機剪到我這樣的畫面，跟當時在講什麼完全無關。

Content: Rice: It just meant that a lot of people came out of the woodwork that were like, don't be sad, we love the game.
Content_zh: Rice：結果就是很多平常沒出聲的人都跑出來說：不要難過，我們很喜歡這款遊戲。

Content: And don't listen to the haters.
Content_zh: 不要理那些酸民。

Content: Kipnis: Wait, wait. Can you send this link to us?
Content_zh: Kipnis：等等，可以把連結傳給我們嗎？

Content: Rice: Yeah. If you looked at forum threads, the main forum threads.
Content_zh: Rice：可以。如果你去看論壇串，尤其是主要的論壇串。

Content: Schafer: What did you do last week?
Content_zh: Schafer：你上週做了什麼？

Content: Fish: Face it, baby. You're hooked on me.
Content_zh: Fish：認了吧，寶貝。你已經被我釣上了。

Content: It's even funnier in the game, you guys.
Content_zh: 各位，這在遊戲裡更好笑。

Content: Rice: Spoiler. Schafer: Spoilers.
Content_zh: Rice：劇透。Schafer：劇透。

Content: [Music] Schafer: I really am trying everything to make sure the game comes out this year.
Content_zh: [音樂] Schafer：我真的什麼都試了，就是想確保遊戲今年能推出。

Content: Rice: It's just going in knowing that that would mean like doing whatever it takes to make that happen.
Content_zh: Rice：只是我們一開始就知道，這代表得不惜一切讓它成真。

Content: And sacrificing in some ways.
Content_zh: 某些地方也得做出犧牲。

Content: Schafer: I think we can do December.
Content_zh: Schafer：我覺得十二月可以。

Content: To me, it's like an infinity far away.
Content_zh: 對我來說，那遠得像無限遠。

Content: Schafer: There's like backer expectations.
Content_zh: Schafer：有贊助者的期待。

Content: There is a financial.
Content_zh: 也有財務面的壓力。

Content: And for me, just the possibility of actually being up for awards.
Content_zh: 對我來說，還有真的能角逐獎項的可能。

Content: It's really just the best game that we can make and we feel like we can actually make without crashing the company into the ground.
Content_zh: 重點其實是做出我們能做的最好遊戲，而且不要把公司拖垮。

Content: Rice: And so I guess on the screen right now is the current timeline for production on Act Two.
Content_zh: Rice：所以現在螢幕上的是第二幕目前的製作時程。

Content: This is what we're working towards and this was the attempt to get to a December launch.
Content_zh: 這是我們正在努力達成的目標，也就是嘗試在十二月推出。

Content: So like even this is stuff that is a real challenge and a lot of people feel, on the team, it's going to take everything, everyone firing like on all cylinders.
Content_zh: 光是這樣就已經很有挑戰，團隊很多人都覺得得全力以赴，每個人都火力全開。

Content: Everything falling into the right places.
Content_zh: 每件事都得剛好到位。

Content: But seems doable with a push.
Content_zh: 但如果衝一波，看起來做得到。

Content: And so that is the other challenge, is that all of this requires like our team coming back and working in full, having writing ready to go.
Content_zh: 另一個挑戰是，這一切都需要團隊回到完整狀態，也需要劇本準備好。

Content: Schafer: On Broken Age? OK, Broken Age.
Content_zh: Schafer：《Broken Age》？好吧，《Broken Age》。

Content: We have people coming back.
Content_zh: 有人要回來了。

Content: Ray's coming back. Crook: Tomorrow.
Content_zh: Ray 要回來了。Crook：明天。

Content: Schafer: You're typing something.
Content_zh: Schafer：你在打字。

Content: You're on Facebook over there.
Content_zh: 你在那邊上 Facebook。

Content: Crook: Just with my eyes.
Content_zh: Crook：只用眼睛而已。

Content: Schafer: Facebooking.
Content_zh: Schafer：滑 Facebook。

Content: Ray's coming back tomorrow.
Content_zh: Ray 明天回來。

Content: And Rusty's been back.
Content_zh: Rusty 也已經回來了。

Content: Anna's been wiring up actual puzzles in Act Two, right?
Content_zh: Anna 一直在把第二幕真正的謎題串起來，對吧？

Content: Crook: Here we go.
Content_zh: Crook：好了。

Content: Offscreen Voice: Been a while?
Content_zh: 畫外音：有一陣子了？

Content: Crook: Deja vu. That's good.
Content_zh: Crook：既視感。這樣不錯。

Content: It's good to be back on, back in the world of Shay and Vella.
Content_zh: 能回來，回到 Shay 和 Vella 的世界，感覺很好。

Content: So I took, took a little time off away from it, not off Double Fine, but just away from the Broken Age and helped out on a couple of other projects.
Content_zh: 所以我離開了一小段時間，不是離開 Double Fine，而是暫時離開《Broken Age》，去幫忙幾個其他專案。

Content: And that was great, because it was just totally different and just a new, it was a nice break.
Content_zh: 那很棒，因為內容完全不一樣，很新鮮，是很好的轉換。

Content: Schafer: Wow, the team is big again.
Content_zh: Schafer：哇，團隊又變大了。

Content: Look at that.
Content_zh: 你看。

Content: And Oliver's working.
Content_zh: Oliver 也在工作。

Content: Franzke: We're implementing puzzles and scenes are getting drawn and like sample scripts are getting written, so it's, it's pretty good.
Content_zh: Franzke：我們在實作謎題，場景也在畫，範例腳本也在寫，所以情況還不錯。

Content: We got Ben back now as well on the project, which is good, exactly.
Content_zh: Ben 現在也回到這個專案了，這很好，沒錯。

Content: Peck: Wow. That was a roller coaster, like now we're going to have to do the whole thing over again in less time.
Content_zh: Peck：哇，那真像雲霄飛車。現在我們得用更少時間把整件事再做一次。

Content: Kipnis: Yeah, I guess at this point it's one of these things where we know that we'll be able to do the game.
Content_zh: Kipnis：對，我想到了這個階段，我們知道遊戲做得出來。

Content: The matter will be to the standard that we have in Act One, like that's not a risk however.
Content_zh: 問題是能不能做到第一幕的水準，不過那不是風險。

Content: That's not a concern that we have.
Content_zh: 那不是我們擔心的事。

Content: But we just like, now it's like OK, can we do this in a few months?
Content_zh: 只是現在變成：好，我們能在幾個月內做到嗎？

Content: And we'll see.
Content_zh: 我們等著瞧吧。

Content: Hansen: Yeah, we worked through a lot of the kinks that we experienced during the Act One, so it's really just about pure execution at this point.
Content_zh: Hansen：對，第一幕遇到的很多問題我們都摸過了，所以現在基本上就是執行。

Content: Petty: This wouldn't stay here, but if you had like everything on, so they kind of don't overlap each other
Content_zh: Petty：這不會留在這裡，但如果所有東西都開著，讓它們不要彼此重疊，

Content: but they all add up versus like this stuff.
Content_zh: 而是全部疊加起來，不像這些東西。

Content: Schafer: Cool. That's neat.
Content_zh: Schafer：酷，很好。

Content: Petty: Since it's the only thing that's really changed, hopefully it becomes like the player knows it's a focal point, you know.
Content_zh: Petty：因為它是唯一真正有變化的東西，希望玩家會知道那是焦點。

Content: They know they need to keep going back, 'cause it's different.
Content_zh: 他們會知道要一直回去看，因為它不一樣了。

Content: Peck: I'm pretty excited about how much of the tech we already have in place and we can just like hit the ground running on
Content_zh: Peck：我很興奮的是，我們已經有很多技術到位了，可以一開始就直接衝。

Content: just writing up the game play and stuff like that.
Content_zh: 直接開始把 gameplay 寫起來之類的。

Content: Crook: So that maybe makes it a little less exciting, in a way, just because it's not new.
Content_zh: Crook：所以某方面來說，這也許比較沒那麼刺激，因為不是全新的東西。

Content: As far as figuring things out, but it also makes you less nervous, you know, 'cause we know, OK, it worked.
Content_zh: 就摸索而言是這樣，但也會比較不緊張，因為我們知道：好，這套可行。

Content: And you know, there's a few things we want to change.
Content_zh: 當然，也有幾件事我們想改。

Content: Crook: We really need to work on the flip book interface.
Content_zh: Crook：我們真的得處理翻頁書介面。

Content: It's not very intuitive.
Content_zh: 不太直覺。

Content: Another thing.
Content_zh: 還有一件事。

Content: Is there any way, Oliver, I know we've mentioned this in the past, but faster exporting of anims from Maya? Yeah.
Content_zh: Oliver，有沒有辦法，我知道以前提過，就是讓 Maya 匯出動畫更快一點？對。

Content: It's just, you know, it's just these, this, we have a lot of big scenes.
Content_zh: 只是你知道，我們有很多大場景。

Content: Tim writes long scenes and they, and you make one change, you test it like, ahh, OK, that's good.
Content_zh: Tim 會寫很長的場景，你改一個東西，測一下，覺得好，這樣可以。

Content: Then you got to re-export again, that's another ten, fifteen minutes.
Content_zh: 然後又得重新匯出，又是十到十五分鐘。

Content: Franzke: I mean, one thing I'm interested to investigate is whether we could launch Maya in batch mode somehow
Content_zh: Franzke：我是滿想研究一件事，就是能不能用某種方式以批次模式啟動 Maya，

Content: and like execute a script on it.
Content_zh: 然後在裡面執行腳本。

Content: Petty: I would if we have a network machine that does it.
Content_zh: Petty：如果有一台連在網路上的機器能做，我會這麼做。

Content: Just like when, you know, in Brutal Legend we eventually stopped doing local exports of the movie
Content_zh: 就像在《Brütal Legend》時，我們後來不再在本機匯出影片，

Content: and started having the build machine to do it, yeah.
Content_zh: 改成讓建置機器去做，對。

Content: It would also help if we didn't have, you know, seven year old machines with only 4 gigs of RAM.
Content_zh: 如果我們用的不是七年前、只有 4GB RAM 的機器，也會有幫助。

Content: So it could be fixed by, you know, I don't know.
Content_zh: 所以也許可以靠，我不知道。

Content: Crook: Greg. Buy all new computers.
Content_zh: Crook：Greg，買一批新電腦吧。

Content: Crook: So it'll, it'll just make workflow easier.
Content_zh: Crook：這樣就會讓工作流程更順。

Content: I think it'll save us a lot of time in bug fixing.
Content_zh: 我覺得會省下很多修 bug 的時間。

Content: Petty: You know, I noticed that there are a lot of animation bugs as the production went on.
Content_zh: Petty：我注意到製作越往後，動畫 bug 變得很多。

Content: And some of those, a lot of those boil down to like sorting issues.
Content_zh: 其中很多其實都歸結到排序問題。

Content: Crook: Basically one piece of geometry didn't know whether it should sort in front or in back of another piece of geometry.
Content_zh: Crook：基本上就是某個幾何物件不知道自己該排在另一個幾何物件前面還是後面。

Content: Petty: The way we've done sorting to support all the platforms is limited and they spend a lot of time cleaning that up.
Content_zh: Petty：我們為了支援所有平台所做的排序方式有限制，他們花很多時間在清理那些問題。

Content: Like if we knew that we were only making it for a high-end console ever, we'd probably have had different solutions
Content_zh: 如果我們一開始就知道只做高階主機版，可能會有不同的解法，

Content: that would have removed some of the burden off the animators or artists, but you know, when you're developing a new engine and you're supporting
Content_zh: 可以減輕動畫師或美術的一些負擔。但你知道，開發新引擎時又要支援……

Content: a really wide array of platforms, you can't really do that.
Content_zh: 非常多種平台，就沒辦法那樣做。

Content: Or at least it's harder.
Content_zh: 至少會更難。

Content: Franzke: Yeah, gosh.
Content_zh: Franzke：是啊，天哪。

Content: Petty: What do you think, Oliver?
Content_zh: Petty：你覺得呢，Oliver？

Content: This is way more fun that fixing compatibility errors.
Content_zh: 這比修相容性錯誤有趣多了。

Content: Rice: Yep. So I mean, Ben, you're on some stuff now and I guess a lot of this would be coming your way.
Content_zh: Rice：對。所以 Ben，你現在有在做一些東西，我想很多這類事情會到你手上。

Content: Franzke: How do you feel about crunching?
Content_zh: Franzke：你對加班趕工有什麼感覺？

Content: Petty: I mean it's always a balance for every engine I've been on, 'cause all the games I've worked on I've never used commercial licensed engines.
Content_zh: Petty：我做過的每個引擎都要取捨，因為我做過的遊戲從來沒用過商用授權引擎。

Content: They've always been developed in-house.
Content_zh: 一直都是自家開發。

Content: So there's always this balance between getting the game done and adding tools to make making the game more effective.
Content_zh: 所以永遠都得在完成遊戲，和加入工具讓製作更有效率之間取得平衡。

Content: And it's really hard to know where that balance should be.
Content_zh: 很難知道平衡應該在哪。

Content: Petty: I guess I just wanted to mention that there's a ball of work potentially to like fix some of the workflows that got broken
Content_zh: Petty：我只是想提一下，可能有一大團工作要做，像是修一些壞掉的工作流程，

Content: and also improve some things that I never got a chance to improve, to try and also make the content production of Act Two as efficient as possible.
Content_zh: 也改善一些我一直沒機會改善的東西，讓第二幕的內容製作盡可能有效率。

Content: Franzke: Like, this, this, this schedule to some degree also relies in effect just like in Act One
Content_zh: Franzke：像這個時程某種程度上也仰賴一件事，就像第一幕一樣，

Content: we actually have some manpower available to fix and even possibly improve the workflow, which is right now really bad,
Content_zh: 就是我們真的得有人力可以修，甚至改善工作流程。現在真的很糟，

Content: because it just bi-rotted all alway because nobody had time to really look at it.
Content_zh: 因為一直沒人有時間仔細看，它就整個慢慢爛掉了。

Content: Like opening scenes takes insane amount of time, nobody really knows why.
Content_zh: 像是開場景要花超久，沒人真的知道為什麼。

Content: I don't have time to look into it.
Content_zh: 我沒時間查。

Content: Nobody has, Anna needs to work.
Content_zh: 大家都沒時間，Anna 還得趕工作。

Content: We only have two programmers.
Content_zh: 我們只有兩個程式設計師。

Content: Rice: If we're worried about December, that seems like that's going to be something that makes it even more challenging.
Content_zh: Rice：如果我們已經在擔心十二月，那這看起來會讓挑戰更大。

Content: Petty: Just something to throw in the bucket with the rest of it.
Content_zh: Petty：只是再丟一件事進那一大桶問題裡。

Content: Petty: And so we're, we're going to a more traditional production cycle now, trying to actually build the very specific game assets,
Content_zh: Petty：所以我們現在要進入比較傳統的製作週期，真正開始做很明確的遊戲資產，

Content: not just design them, you know.
Content_zh: 不只是設計它們。

Content: Preproduction way.
Content_zh: 不是前期製作那種做法。

Content: And we're finishing our first sprint of Act Two.
Content_zh: 我們也快完成第二幕的第一個 sprint。

Content: [Music] Schafer: This is the first time we've played through since we started Act Two.
Content_zh: [音樂] Schafer：這是我們開始第二幕以來第一次完整跑一次。

Content: Kipnis: Although I need to make sure that the sound is actually working.
Content_zh: Kipnis：不過我得確認聲音真的有正常。

Content: Last time on Broken Age.
Content_zh: 《Broken Age》前情提要。

Content: Schafer: Are we going to have that?
Content_zh: Schafer：我們會放那個嗎？

Content: Oh my God. OK. Here we go.
Content_zh: 我的天。好，來了。

Content: This better not fucking end here!
Content_zh: 最好不要他媽的停在這裡！

Content: It better not be the end!
Content_zh: 最好不要就這樣結束！

Content: I paid $15 for, what?
Content_zh: 我花了十五美元，到底買到什麼？

Content: Pull on, OK.
Content_zh: 拉一下，好。

Content: Ohmygod is it going to go right into it? Awesome.
Content_zh: 天啊，會直接接下去嗎？太棒了。

Content: That's so nice and smooth.
Content_zh: 這樣很順，很漂亮。

Content: So when you play through the game, the only thing is like, do you think we should still put the text up, like "end of Act One"?
Content_zh: 所以玩到這裡時，唯一的問題是，你覺得我們還要放「第一幕結束」那種文字嗎？

Content: Kipnis: No, no, no. It should just go right in.
Content_zh: Kipnis：不用，不用。應該直接接下去。

Content: Schafer: You guys seem to have already come to an agreement about this one.
Content_zh: Schafer：你們好像已經對這件事達成共識了。

Content: Kipnis: I mean we could, but it wouldn't be as...
Content_zh: Kipnis：我是說可以，但就不會那麼……

Content: Schafer: Should I pick the girl?
Content_zh: Schafer：要我選那個女孩嗎？

Content: Kipnis: No. Don't do it.
Content_zh: Kipnis：不要。別選。

Content: Petty: It'd be a very short play through.
Content_zh: Petty：那會變成很短的通關流程。

Content: Schafer: It's like, oh no! Horrible bug.
Content_zh: Schafer：就像，喔不！超可怕的 bug。

Content: Kipnis: He needs a runs.
Content_zh: Kipnis：他需要跑步動畫。

Content: Petty: You know what I realized, too, is Shay's in all these scenes.
Content_zh: Petty：你知道我也發現什麼嗎？Shay 在這些場景裡，

Content: He feels a bit small in all of them.
Content_zh: 每一幕都感覺有點小。

Content: He's the correct scale, but he's actually, he's so much smaller feeling than Vella.
Content_zh: 比例是對的，但他的感覺真的比 Vella 小很多。

Content: Their height is about the same, but because she has the bigger hair and a wider face.
Content_zh: 他們身高差不多，但她頭髮比較大，臉也比較寬。

Content: Like he looks small talking to the same characters as if you, like when you just, and I've doubled, triple checked the scale and it's all correct.
Content_zh: 他跟同樣的角色說話時看起來就很小。我已經反覆檢查比例，全部都正確。

Content: It just, Kipnis: I wonder if we should make him bigger.
Content_zh: 只是……Kipnis：不知道是不是該把他放大。

Content: Petty: Well, it'd throw off all the stuff at this point.
Content_zh: Petty：可是現在改會把一堆東西都打亂。

Content: Schafer: So I assume, I think Lydia is awkward.
Content_zh: Schafer：所以我猜，現在這有點尷尬。

Content: She came 'cause I think she thinks this is like, yeah, a surprise going away party.
Content_zh: 她會來，是因為她大概以為這是驚喜歡送會。

Content: She's coming, is this where, is this where the cake is, you guys?
Content_zh: 她來了。蛋糕是在這裡嗎，各位？

Content: Is this where we're having the big going away?
Content_zh: 盛大的歡送會是在這裡嗎？

Content: This is Lydia's last play-through before she goes to...
Content_zh: 這是 Lydia 去……之前最後一次一起完整試跑。

Content: That's Oculus. That's my gesture for Oculus.
Content_zh: 那是 Oculus。這是我的 Oculus 手勢。

Content: Facehugger.
Content_zh: 抱臉蟲。

Content: Anyone have any last words for Lydia?
Content_zh: 有人要對 Lydia 說臨別贈言嗎？

Content: Petty: Thank you for all your hard work, Lydia.
Content_zh: Petty：Lydia，謝謝你一直以來的辛苦。

Content: [Applause] Kipnis: All your v-effects in Act One were amazing
Content_zh: [掌聲] Kipnis：你在第一幕做的所有 VFX 都很棒，

Content: and we hope we get somebody anywhere near as good as you for Act Two.
Content_zh: 希望第二幕能找到至少接近你水準的人。

Content: Crook: Yeah. Yeah, that was unfortunate.
Content_zh: Crook：對，對，那真的很可惜。

Content: Good old Lydia.
Content_zh: 可靠的 Lydia。

Content: The turnover here is very, is not high.
Content_zh: 這裡的人員流動率很……不高。

Content: It's actually, it's not high at all.
Content_zh: 其實一點也不高。

Content: And I think sometimes we're lucky to have people for as long as we do.
Content_zh: 所以我覺得有時候人能留這麼久，我們已經很幸運了。

Content: And it is a little scary, like we were talking about effects today, a little bit.
Content_zh: 但也有點可怕，像今天我們就稍微談到特效。

Content: Then I was, you know, and then in the back of your head, like wait.
Content_zh: 然後我腦中就有個聲音：等等。

Content: Who's going to do these?
Content_zh: 誰會做這些？

Content: I know Lee was doing a little bit of effects work the other day, but he's not going to have time to like, be the effects person.
Content_zh: 我知道 Lee 前幾天有做一點特效，但他不會有時間真的擔任特效那個人。

Content: So I'm just going to have to trust that they can find somebody.
Content_zh: 所以我得相信他們能找到人。

Content: 'Cause we do have, we have a couple other effects artists, but they're on other projects.
Content_zh: 因為我們確實還有幾位特效美術，但他們都在其他專案上。

Content: So we're going to have to find somebody new that can come in and try and match that style and we will, we will.
Content_zh: 所以我們得找新人進來，試著接上那個風格。我們會的，會找到的。

Content: And so, so we'll find somebody that can do it.
Content_zh: 所以，我們會找到能做的人。

Content: So I'm not freaking out, but I don't know.
Content_zh: 所以我沒有崩潰，只是也說不準。

Content: She was great.
Content_zh: 她很棒。

Content: Schafer: OK. But it's awesome.
Content_zh: Schafer：好。不過很棒。

Content: See those puzzles coming together. Kipnis: Yeah.
Content_zh: 看到那些謎題成形。Kipnis：對。

Content: Schafer: We'll be done by December!
Content_zh: Schafer：我們十二月就會做完！

Content: Rice: Exactly. Petty: It's nice that you know when...
Content_zh: Rice：沒錯。Petty：知道什麼時候……還不錯。

Content: Schafer: This is where everyone jumps out and is like, yeah!
Content_zh: Schafer：這時大家就會跳出來說：耶！

Content: Schafer: People were worried about it being done in December, earlier and then the writing is taking even a little bit longer than we thought.
Content_zh: Schafer：之前大家就擔心十二月能不能完成，結果劇本又比我們想的多花了一點時間。

Content: So I bet people were even more worried about it being done in December.
Content_zh: 所以我猜大家更擔心十二月做不做得完。

Content: Schafer: But we did not quite finish the work, the milestone and when we started the milestone
Content_zh: Schafer：但我們沒有完全完成這個里程碑的工作，而一開始這個里程碑時，

Content: we're like, from now on if we don't finish milestones we're just going to cut stuff and when we don't finish them.
Content_zh: 我們說從現在開始，如果里程碑沒完成，就直接砍內容。結果現在沒完成。

Content: And the milestone, so I guess we're just going to have to cut half of those puzzles.
Content_zh: 所以我想，我們只好砍掉那些謎題的一半。

Content: So we're going to go back to that meeting we had where we said that we're going to cut stuff and be like, OK, listen.
Content_zh: 所以我們得回到那場說要砍內容的會議，然後說，好，聽著。

Content: It's not totally reasonable to just cut stuff that doesn't get done.
Content_zh: 沒做完就直接砍，其實不完全合理。

Content: We're going to just have to make up for it somehow.
Content_zh: 我們得想辦法補回來。

Content: Petty: The intent was never to be, oh, we didn't finish that puzzle.
Content_zh: Petty：原本的意思從來不是，喔，這個謎題沒做完，

Content: We're just not doing it.
Content_zh: 那我們就不做了。

Content: It was, it was that we didn't want to leave a wake of unfinished, half-implemented puzzles for a variety of reasons
Content_zh: 而是基於很多原因，我們不想留下一串未完成、只做一半的謎題。

Content: and one was you can't play-test a half-implemented puzzle meaningfully.
Content_zh: 其中一個原因是，半成品謎題沒辦法有意義地試玩測試。

Content: And we wanted to play-test sooner to help ameliorate the amount of like play-tested adjustments at the end
Content_zh: 我們也想早點試玩測試，減少最後才根據測試結果調整的量，

Content: when we have less flexibility, 'cause the dialogue's recorded and we're running out of time.
Content_zh: 因為到時候彈性比較少，對話已經錄好，時間也快沒了。

Content: So I mean, it's all those reasons why we're trying to do that.
Content_zh: 所以我們想那麼做，是出於這些原因。

Content: But what actually wound up happening for a couple reasons was that we didn't hit those two puzzles.
Content_zh: 但實際上，因為幾個原因，我們那兩個謎題沒有達標。

Content: So we're effectively, I don't know, a week or so behind already on the schedule in our first two-week sprint.
Content_zh: 所以在第一個兩週 sprint，我們實際上已經落後差不多一週。

Content: And it's going to continue, like we'll continue to get further behind unless we can close out puzzles on that sprint.
Content_zh: 而且會繼續下去。除非我們能在 sprint 內收掉謎題，不然會越落後越多。

Content: And I don't see anything that changes that.
Content_zh: 我看不出有什麼能改變這點。

Content: Right now, for example, there's really only one puzzle that's finished writing.
Content_zh: 例如現在，真的只有一個謎題的劇本寫完。

Content: We can't finish the hull patch puzzle chain, because it's missing writing.
Content_zh: 我們不能完成船殼補丁謎題鏈，因為缺劇本。

Content: We can't finish the hexapal wiring, we can't finish radiation suit.
Content_zh: Hexipal 接線做不完，防輻射衣也做不完。

Content: So that still means we're behind.
Content_zh: 那仍然代表我們落後了。

Content: Schafer: ok.
Content_zh: Schafer：好吧。

Content: Hansen: Would that allow us to potentially catch up, if we got the writing in time?
Content_zh: Hansen：如果劇本及時到位，那有可能讓我們追上嗎？

Content: Schafer: So I bet I'm maybe the last person who thinks we can get it done in December, probably.
Content_zh: Schafer：我猜我可能是最後一個還覺得十二月做得完的人。

Content: You can kind of sense when you're in a position where everyone kind of thinks you're not going to make the deadline, but they just haven't told you yet.
Content_zh: 有時你會感覺到，大家其實都覺得你趕不上期限，只是還沒告訴你。

Content: So I'm going to go ahead and pretend I haven't heard it yet until they, it's just too much time has gone by, they can't tell me.
Content_zh: 所以我就繼續假裝還沒聽到，拖到時間過太久，他們也沒辦法再告訴我。

Content: Then they won't tell me.
Content_zh: 然後他們就不會說了。

Content: And then we'll make it on time, see?
Content_zh: 然後我們就會準時完成，看吧？

Content: That's how I operate.
Content_zh: 我就是這樣運作的。

Content: Kind of mind games.
Content_zh: 有點像心理戰。

Content: Hansen: There're two different scenarios that we can do pretty much.
Content_zh: Hansen：基本上我們可以做兩種不同情境。

Content: Well, one is we don't ship in December.
Content_zh: 一個是我們十二月不推出。

Content: I mean, that's probably the one we don't want to go for.
Content_zh: 我是說，這大概是我們不想選的。

Content: And the other one, basically eats into Beta periods so that we won't have a true Beta.
Content_zh: 另一個基本上會吃掉 Beta 階段，讓我們沒有真正的 Beta。

Content: We would actually have animations and v-effects and a lot of the audio stuff coming in late, like into Beta.
Content_zh: 動畫、VFX，還有很多音效內容都會很晚才進來，甚至進到 Beta 才進來。

Content: And there's a lot of risk involved in doing that.
Content_zh: 這樣做有很多風險。

Content: So that was something that we were trying to avoid.
Content_zh: 所以那是我們一直想避免的事。

Content: Rice: Then it's like getting the writing done so we can have a VO session in time to where we get the files back soon enough to get our animators
Content_zh: Rice：接著就是要把劇本完成，讓我們能及時安排 VO 錄音，早點拿回檔案，好讓動畫師……

Content: pumping through that from Super Genius, but also so it's not late,
Content_zh: 從 Super Genius 那邊開始消化那些內容，而且不能太晚。

Content: too late to where we're screwing audio and the effects guys.
Content_zh: 不能晚到害音效和特效的人都被卡住。

Content: So there's just kind of a balance in there.
Content_zh: 所以中間就是要取得平衡。

Content: Hansen: We presented Tim with a really aggressive schedule for delivering the writing that we needed in order to keep the team on schedule.
Content_zh: Hansen：我們給 Tim 一個很激進的劇本交付時程，因為那是讓團隊照進度走所需要的。

Content: And it was requiring him to work at a faster pace than he's ever written on the projects up to this point.
Content_zh: 那要求他用比目前為止任何專案都更快的速度寫。

Content: It's a challenge for everybody, but it all starts with the writings.
Content_zh: 這對所有人都是挑戰，但一切都從劇本開始。

Content: Annable: So Tim, did you see the email that I sent you where I said I just did this little exercise.
Content_zh: Annable：Tim，你有看到我寄給你的信嗎？我說我做了一個小推算。

Content: Schafer: Yeah, I deleted that one immediately.
Content_zh: Schafer：對，我立刻把那封刪了。

Content: It was terrifying.
Content_zh: 太可怕了。

Content: That was the one where you were like...
Content_zh: 就是你說……的那封。

Content: it looks he spent a year writing this first one, so...
Content_zh: 看起來他第一幕寫了一年，所以……

Content: Annable: No, no. And it's, no. 'Cause you didn't take a year; you spent like 13 weeks and to me, you know, we don't,
Content_zh: Annable：不，不。其實不是。因為你沒有花一年，你大概花了十三週。對我來說，我們不能，

Content: we don't really succeed when we say hey, Tim, write faster.
Content_zh: 我們不能只是說：嘿 Tim，寫快一點，就真的解決問題。

Content: It's kind of an impossible thing to do.
Content_zh: 那基本上是強人所難。

Content: So for me, I just don't see December as a, as a reality.
Content_zh: 所以對我來說，我真的不覺得十二月是現實可行的。

Content: Rice: So I don't know if we want to make a call now or if we want to say let's try to write the rest of the game
Content_zh: Rice：所以我不知道我們是要現在做決定，還是說再試著在四週半內……

Content: in four and a half weeks and still squeeze it in.
Content_zh: 把剩下的遊戲寫完，然後硬塞進去。

Content: Annable: I just worry about the quality then.
Content_zh: Annable：我只是擔心品質。

Content: I mean, because even if he, Tim, even if you locked yourself away and wrote for a month, you know, I mean that's a lot of pressure.
Content_zh: 因為就算，Tim，就算你把自己關起來寫一個月，那壓力也非常大。

Content: Schafer: Mmhmm.
Content_zh: Schafer：嗯。

Content: Annable: I mean, Tim, do you think you can do that?
Content_zh: Annable：我的意思是，Tim，你認為你能做到嗎？

Content: Schafer: Again I don't see a lot of choice.
Content_zh: Schafer：我還是覺得沒什麼選擇。

Content: Annable: I mean, it'll be a gnarly schedule for everybody.
Content_zh: Annable：我是說，那對大家都會是很硬的時程。

Content: Hansen: All right. So why don't we create those scenarios and then we can share the, once this group, and we can talk about it from there.
Content_zh: Hansen：好。那我們就建立那些情境，然後分享給這個小組，再從那裡開始討論。

Content: Schafer: Well, I better get writing. Rice: Yeah.
Content_zh: Schafer：好吧，我最好去寫劇本了。Rice：對。

Content: Annable: All right. Whoohoo! You can do it, Tim. You can do it.
Content_zh: Annable：好。唷呼！你可以的，Tim。你可以的。

Content: Schafer: OK. Annable: OK.
Content_zh: Schafer：好。Annable：好。

Content: Schafer: All right. Thanks for talking.
Content_zh: Schafer：好，謝謝你們聊這個。

Content: I wish I had more easy solutions for everybody.
Content_zh: 希望我能給大家更多簡單的解法。

Content: Annable: Bye, guys. Schafer: Bye.
Content_zh: Annable：大家掰。Schafer：掰。

Content: Rice: Hi, Malena. OK.
Content_zh: Rice：嗨，Malena。好。

Content: I'm getting Bagel, too.
Content_zh: 我也要叫 Bagel。

Content: We'll see if he comes.
Content_zh: 看看他來不來。

Content: Tim is not coming today, you guys.
Content_zh: 各位，Tim 今天不會來。

Content: Kipnis: Why not?
Content_zh: Kipnis：為什麼？

Content: Rice: He is in his office with a big sticker that says "Do Not Disturb".
Content_zh: Rice：他在辦公室，門上貼了一張大貼紙寫著「請勿打擾」。

Content: Melena says.
Content_zh: Malena 是這麼說的。

Content: So he's writing.
Content_zh: 所以他在寫劇本。

Content: No, he's actually, he's been killing it.
Content_zh: 不過說真的，他最近超猛。

Content: He's been cranking through stuff really quick.
Content_zh: 他很快就把東西一個個寫出來。

Content: I think he sees the deadlines looming and is staying really on top of stuff.
Content_zh: 我覺得他看到期限逼近，所以把事情盯得很緊。

Content: So I think if he keeps going at this pace, then yeah, we're going to have a good shot at it.
Content_zh: 所以如果他能維持這個速度，我覺得我們真的很有機會。

Content: Yeah, he's doing his best and it's coming in really nice.
Content_zh: 對，他很努力，而且交出來的東西很不錯。

Content: It's solid now, so it's good to see.
Content_zh: 現在很紮實了，看了很開心。

Content: Schafer: As long as we can survive 'til the end of the project, it'll be great.
Content_zh: Schafer：只要我們能撐到專案結束，就會很棒。

Content: I guess I gotta finish my dialog before...
Content_zh: 我想我得先把對話寫完，才……

Content: You guys get to talk to me ever again.
Content_zh: 你們才有機會再跟我講話。

Content: Schafer: Out. Out!
Content_zh: Schafer：出去。出去！

Content: Just kidding.
Content_zh: 開玩笑的。

Content: Come in any time you want to.
Content_zh: 你們想來隨時都可以。

Content: Is that a good enough ending?
Content_zh: 這個收尾夠好嗎？

Content: I'll do like this.
Content_zh: 我就這樣做。

Content: I guess it's just another day in games.
Content_zh: 我想這就是遊戲業普通的一天。

Content: Schafer: Right, right?
Content_zh: Schafer：對吧？

Content: I can close my door and I can put a Post-it note on it and I can even lock it.
Content_zh: 我可以把門關上，貼張便利貼，甚至可以鎖門。

Content: But just walking around the office, people will be like, ah, I need you to talk, I need to talk to you about this thing.
Content_zh: 但只要在辦公室走一圈，就會有人說：啊，我需要你，我要跟你談這件事。

Content: And then that thing spins into another thing.
Content_zh: 然後那件事又扯出另一件事。

Content: And someone wants to talk about another thing.
Content_zh: 又有人想談另一件事。

Content: So many projects going on, so many deals that I probably should be helping with and you know, they justifiably need some input or something.
Content_zh: 公司有這麼多專案、這麼多合作案，我大概也應該幫忙，而他們也確實需要一些意見之類的。

Content: But I just have to get this writing done.
Content_zh: 可是我就是得把這些劇本寫完。

Content: I just have to get this writing done.
Content_zh: 我就是得把這些劇本寫完。

Content: So everything else in the company's got to, got to operate on its own for a little while.
Content_zh: 所以公司其他所有事情都得暫時自己運作一陣子。

Content: Schafer: We're here at the Noe Valley Public Library.
Content_zh: Schafer：我們現在在 Noe Valley 公共圖書館。

Content: My house is a few blocks down that way.
Content_zh: 我家就在那邊幾條街外。

Content: I've come to libraries ever since trying to design Full Throttle.
Content_zh: 從設計《Full Throttle》開始，我就會來圖書館。

Content: I remember going down to the Sausalito Library and sitting there and designing.
Content_zh: 我記得我會去 Sausalito 圖書館，坐在那裡設計。

Content: Sitting there with my notebook, same exact thing.
Content_zh: 拿著筆記本坐在那裡，完全一樣。

Content: And Grim I went to the library a lot.
Content_zh: 做《Grim Fandango》時，我也常去圖書館。

Content: I actually did a lot of research in the library, just 'cause the Internet was not full of stuff as it is now.
Content_zh: 我真的在圖書館做了很多研究，因為當時網路上還不像現在什麼都有。

Content: It's also nice to get out of the office.
Content_zh: 離開辦公室也滿好的。

Content: It's a change of scenery.
Content_zh: 換個環境。

Content: I used to think like, on Full Throttle, I went to the library and I got so much done on the first day.
Content_zh: 以前做《Full Throttle》時，我去圖書館，第一天完成超多事。

Content: I was like, this is great, this is my new secret.
Content_zh: 我就覺得，太棒了，這是我的新祕訣。

Content: And then I went down the next day and I didn't get any work done.
Content_zh: 結果隔天再去，什麼事都沒做成。

Content: It was like, what's going on?
Content_zh: 到底怎麼回事？

Content: And I got a lot of work done in the office.
Content_zh: 有時候我在辦公室完成很多工作。

Content: And then sometimes I get a lot of work done in my home.
Content_zh: 有時候在家也完成很多。

Content: And sometimes I think there's no one answer.
Content_zh: 所以有時候我覺得沒有唯一答案。

Content: You just have to keep moving around and shuffling around.
Content_zh: 你就是得一直換地方、到處試。

Content: And I think in the end it really in some ways doesn't matter.
Content_zh: 到最後，某種程度上其實不重要。

Content: You just have to like work at it every day and some days you get nothing done, some days you get a lot done.
Content_zh: 你就是每天都得做。有些日子什麼都做不出來，有些日子會做很多。

Content: But you have to do it every day.
Content_zh: 但你必須每天都做。

Content: [Music] Schafer: Yeah, it's coming along really fast.
Content_zh: [音樂] Schafer：對，進展真的很快。

Content: I mean, they really are working twice as fast as Act One.
Content_zh: 他們真的比第一幕快兩倍。

Content: And we don't have everyone that we had in Act One.
Content_zh: 而且我們沒有第一幕時的完整人手。

Content: Remember in Act One we had like JP and Brandon, all these people.
Content_zh: 記得第一幕時我們有 JP、Brandon，還有那些人。

Content: This one's been on the gameplay side just like Anna and Ben cranking away on it.
Content_zh: 這次在 gameplay 這邊主要就是 Anna 和 Ben 一直猛做。

Content: [Music] Rice: There's certain puzzles we should make sure to hit first.
Content_zh: [音樂] Rice：有些謎題我們應該確定先處理。

Content: Petty: I think we should hit the stuff that we played the least first, which is the radiation suit puzzle.
Content_zh: Petty：我覺得應該先處理我們玩最少的部分，也就是防輻射衣謎題。

Content: I would do that one first. Rice: Cool.
Content_zh: 我會先做那個。Rice：酷。

Content: Schafer: Sprint kickoff.
Content_zh: Schafer：Sprint 啟動會。

Content: I missed the play-through but I heard it was good.
Content_zh: 我錯過了完整試跑，但聽說很不錯。

Content: Rice: The play-through was awesome.
Content_zh: Rice：完整試跑超棒。

Content: Did anybody have anything they wanted to say about it?
Content_zh: 有人想說些什麼嗎？

Content: Schafer: It was particularly awesome because I wasn't here.
Content_zh: Schafer：特別棒是因為我不在。

Content: Like everyone's like, it seems so relaxed.
Content_zh: 大家都說好像很放鬆。

Content: Petty: That was like a plus.
Content_zh: Petty：那算是加分。

Content: Schafer: Why is it so chill here?
Content_zh: Schafer：這裡怎麼這麼 chill？

Content: I don't know what's going on.
Content_zh: 我不知道怎麼回事。

Content: Petty: I mean, I think it was pretty solid.
Content_zh: Petty：我是覺得滿紮實的。

Content: I mean, the puzzles were, they became clear in the play-through that the puzzles were a lot more complex than the first act, which was cool.
Content_zh: 在完整試跑裡很明顯看得出來，這些謎題比第一幕複雜很多，這點很酷。

Content: Game Tester: This is a good puzzle.
Content_zh: 遊戲測試員：這謎題不錯。

Content: I have no idea what's going on.
Content_zh: 我完全不知道怎麼回事。

Content: It's going to be hard.
Content_zh: 會很難。

Content: You need a pen there, Ben?
Content_zh: Ben，你需要筆嗎？

Content: Peck: Yeah, I got one.
Content_zh: Peck：不用，我有。

Content: Petty: Because there are more steps in them and the puzzles, a lot of them aren't just say using an object in a place,
Content_zh: Petty：因為裡面步驟更多，而且很多謎題不是單純把物品用在某個地方，

Content: but there's some trial and error things you can manipulate and see different results.
Content_zh: 而是有一些可以反覆試、可以操控，並看到不同結果的東西。

Content: I think the separate puzzle chains kind of weave in and out of each other.
Content_zh: 我覺得不同的謎題鏈會彼此交織。

Content: So I think that, that's where some of the, I think some people would describe that as difficulty,
Content_zh: 所以我覺得有些人會把那描述成難度，

Content: which is true but I think it's also just the appearance.
Content_zh: 這也沒錯，但我覺得那也只是表象。

Content: And, and actual complexity that was maybe missing from some of the puzzles in the first act.
Content_zh: 還有第一幕某些謎題也許缺少的真正複雜度。

Content: And part of that was by design, both to ramp it up.
Content_zh: 其中一部分是刻意設計的，為了把難度往上拉。

Content: But also, you know, Tim, I think in both cases from a story standpoint or from a theme standpoint,
Content_zh: 但 Tim 在兩邊，不管從故事還是主題角度來看，

Content: the character's kind of start constrained and therefore the player's kind of constrained.
Content_zh: 角色一開始都有點受限，所以玩家也跟著受限。

Content: And they kind of break out of that constraint.
Content_zh: 然後他們會逐漸突破那個限制。

Content: And that means that it was just a little more, there wasn't much opportunity for the puzzles to kind of weave back and forth through each other.
Content_zh: 也就是說，之前比較沒有機會讓謎題彼此來回交織。

Content: Kipnis: So what are your thoughts, you guys?
Content_zh: Kipnis：你們有什麼想法？

Content: Rice: It's fucking awesome. So good.
Content_zh: Rice：他媽的超棒。真的很讚。

Content: Kipnis: It's really long. Peck: Yeah.
Content_zh: Kipnis：它真的很長。Peck：對。

Content: Rice: It's super fun.
Content_zh: Rice：超好玩。

Content: Peck: I really love that last puzzle. Rice: Yeah.
Content_zh: Peck：我真的很喜歡最後那個謎題。Rice：對。

Content: I think it's just like for my level it feels like what we're hoping, which is like much less linear and spread out
Content_zh: 我覺得以我那關來說，它感覺就像我們希望的那樣：比較不線性，也比較分散，

Content: and you're having to really kind of retrace your steps a lot.
Content_zh: 玩家真的得常常回頭走。

Content: Petty: So I just realized a flaw, though, right.
Content_zh: Petty：不過我剛想到一個缺陷。

Content: Like if you are playing the girl and you take her all the way to the end of her chain, and you haven't seen the [spoiler-bleep]
Content_zh: 如果你在玩女孩那邊，把她一路玩到她那條線的最後，但你還沒看過 [劇透消音]，

Content: 'cause they're not part of, we have to make sure they're part of her critical puzzle that you have to see them.
Content_zh: 因為它們不在……我們得確保它們是她關鍵謎題的一部分，玩家一定會看到。

Content: Because I'm thinking about this now.
Content_zh: 因為我現在正在想這件事。

Content: You finished him and you didn't hear [spoiler-bleep], which is what she will need to solve the [spoiler-bleep]
Content_zh: 如果你把他那邊玩完，卻沒聽到 [劇透消音]，而那是她解開 [劇透消音] 需要的東西，

Content: because it's not critical path.
Content_zh: 因為它不在關鍵路徑上。

Content: And there's no way to go back to it, 'cause you're in a scrolling thing.
Content_zh: 而且你沒辦法回去，因為你已經在一個捲動的流程裡。

Content: Rice: And yeah, same with him, same with her where if you've gone all the way through and saw it
Content_zh: Rice：對，他那邊也一樣，她那邊也一樣。如果你一路玩過去看到了，

Content: and just didn't notice it and then now you don't remember the pattern.
Content_zh: 但當下沒注意，現在又不記得那個模式。

Content: Petty: Well, and this one is specifically, you have to be able to go and test it.
Content_zh: Petty：而且這個特別是，你必須能回去測試它。

Content: And once we put you in this mode, you can never go back.
Content_zh: 但一旦我們把你放進這個模式，就再也回不去了。

Content: Peck: Yeah. Petty: So I'm not sure how we solve that.
Content_zh: Peck：對。Petty：所以我不確定這要怎麼解。

Content: Rice: That's a good question for Tim.
Content_zh: Rice：這是個適合問 Tim 的好問題。

Content: Schafer: Oh, this is, I feel like this might be a horrible thing I don't want to think about.
Content_zh: Schafer：喔，這個，我覺得可能是我不想去想的可怕問題。

Content: Petty: Yeah, we're not really sure what to do about this.
Content_zh: Petty：對，我們不知道該怎麼辦。

Content: Schafer: It's just, you have that, it works in play-test.
Content_zh: Schafer：只是你有那個，它在試玩測試裡就可行。

Content: Petty: No! Rice: It doesn't work.
Content_zh: Petty：不行！Rice：它行不通。

Content: Schafer: Now we've made it so that you, Vella and Shay need each other, like you need things from both areas to work in the other areas.
Content_zh: Schafer：現在我們做成 Vella 和 Shay 需要彼此，也就是你需要兩邊區域的東西，才能在另一邊運作。

Content: And we did that on purpose, but then we forget, well, we didn't forget.
Content_zh: 我們是故意這樣做的，但後來忘了，嗯，也不是忘了。

Content: We just failed to remember that you can play one character completely and then like in the first act,
Content_zh: 我們只是沒有想到，玩家可以像第一幕那樣，把一個角色一路玩完，

Content: you could play all of Shay and then go play all of Vella or vice versa.
Content_zh: 先玩完整個 Shay，再去玩整個 Vella，或反過來。

Content: And since we made them interdependent, we thought that wasn't a problem.
Content_zh: 因為我們讓他們互相依賴，所以以為那不是問題。

Content: But if you shut down this whole character and go into that looping animation that means their act is done,
Content_zh: 但如果你讓這個角色整條線結束，進入代表他們那幕已完成的循環動畫，

Content: you can't find the things you need to solve this one.
Content_zh: 你就找不到解決這個謎題所需的東西。

Content: So we're just going to leave that in the unfixable state.
Content_zh: 所以我們就讓它維持在修不了的狀態。

Content: People will be just be like, how far did you get in Broken Age before you couldn't play anymore?
Content_zh: 大家就會問：你玩《Broken Age》玩到哪裡就不能繼續了？

Content: And there'll be like a leader board.
Content_zh: 然後還會有排行榜。

Content: More challenge.
Content_zh: 更有挑戰性。

Content: No more hand holding.
Content_zh: 不再牽著玩家走。

Content: I just haven't figured out a solution to that yet.
Content_zh: 我只是還沒想出解決辦法。

Content: It's not going to be pretty.
Content_zh: 這不會好看。

Content: Schafer: Instead of going into the end state where looping, if you haven't solved one of those puzzles, they go into these other states.
Content_zh: Schafer：如果你還沒解開其中一個謎題，就不要進入那個循環的結束狀態，而是進入其他狀態。

Content: Basically stalling.
Content_zh: 基本上就是拖住。

Content: Rice: At that point it does feel like it's kind of closing people off and a bit handhold-y.
Content_zh: Rice：到了那時候，感覺有點像把玩家擋住，也有點太牽著走。

Content: I don't know. Yeah.
Content_zh: 我不知道。是啊。

Content: Schafer: OK, So we acknowledge that that is a horrible problem for Ben to solve.
Content_zh: Schafer：好，所以我們承認那是 Ben 要解的一個超可怕問題。

Content: Petty: That we're trying to introduce some, more than just story relationships between the separate characters.
Content_zh: Petty：我們想在不同角色之間加入的不只是故事上的關係。

Content: And since we've only really taken Shay's second act to Alpha, the exact solution for how to kind of play on those
Content_zh: 而因為我們其實只把 Shay 的第二幕做到 Alpha，要怎麼利用那些關係，

Content: and that gameplay impact isn't really obvious until we develop more of Vella's side of things.
Content_zh: 以及它對 gameplay 的影響，要等 Vella 那邊做得更多才會清楚。

Content: Rice: I'm really happy with Alpha on the section we just did.
Content_zh: Rice：我們剛做的那段 Alpha，我真的很滿意。

Content: And I don't know, the rest of development is super tight if we're still trying to get December.
Content_zh: 我不知道，如果我們還想拚十二月，接下來的開發會非常緊。

Content: And I think that everybody really wants to make it happen so I think that if we can keep pushing forward it should be,
Content_zh: 我覺得大家真的都想讓它成真，所以如果能繼續往前推，應該……

Content: at least we got a shot right now.
Content_zh: 至少我們現在還有機會。

Content: I think a lot of it's going to come down to like this next month and a half is really important.
Content_zh: 我覺得很多事情都取決於接下來這一個半月，非常重要。

Content: In three, four weeks we'll see where the writing is and hopefully if things look good
Content_zh: 三、四週後我們會看到劇本進度到哪，希望如果情況看起來不錯，

Content: we can rally behind it and push to make it happen.
Content_zh: 大家就能一起卯起來，把它做出來。

Content: Crook: I don't know. December.
Content_zh: Crook：我不知道。十二月。

Content: What are we in, October?
Content_zh: 現在是十月嗎？

Content: November, yeah, no.
Content_zh: 十一月，對，不。

Content: That's not going to happen.
Content_zh: 不可能的。

Content: As far as I can tell.
Content_zh: 就我所知。

Content: But we do not, I have not heard another date.
Content_zh: 但我們沒有，我也沒聽到別的日期。

Content: That's the only date I've heard, but I just think that there's, there's too much work, I think.
Content_zh: 那是我唯一聽到的日期，但我覺得工作太多了。

Content: As far as I can tell, there's way too much work, but that's kind of up to the producers to figure that out.
Content_zh: 就我看來工作真的太多了，不過那就交給製作人去判斷。

Content: [Music] Schafer: The puzzles are great, the writing is late.
Content_zh: [音樂] Schafer：謎題很棒，劇本很慢。

Content: But I've been getting some more done, so everybody shut up.
Content_zh: 但我有繼續寫出一些，所以大家都閉嘴。

Content: Rice: Like we're just not moving at the pace that that timeline has us moving at.
Content_zh: Rice：我們就是沒有照那條時間軸要求的速度前進。

Content: And now that we're getting more and more writing, we're having a better idea of what it's looking like, too.
Content_zh: 現在劇本越來越多，我們也更清楚它大概會是什麼樣子。

Content: But yeah, I think that the conversation of December is still conversation that's happening and one that needs to probably be had further, soon.
Content_zh: 不過對，十二月這件事大家還在談，而且應該很快就需要更深入討論。

Content: 'Cause this is another sprint of like pushing stuff off into the next sprint as we have been.
Content_zh: 因為這又是一個把東西往下一個 sprint 延的 sprint。

Content: Kipnis: The problem with Vella's side is that there are a lot of like mechanical or physical puzzles
Content_zh: Kipnis：Vella 那邊的問題是，有很多偏機械或物理性的謎題，

Content: that are like, I don't know if it's going to communicate what we want.
Content_zh: 我不知道它能不能傳達我們想要的東西。

Content: Like I don't know if it's going to work exactly like how we expect.
Content_zh: 也不知道它會不會真的照我們預期運作。

Content: I just, I worry about the deadline.
Content_zh: 我就是擔心期限。

Content: Rice: I think it's true, I just don't know if it's going to be able to, if it's going to be able to affect the timing or the VO sessions and stuff.
Content_zh: Rice：我覺得沒錯，只是不知道它會不會影響時程，或 VO 錄音之類的。

Content: 'Cause otherwise we're pushing it off to where Camden seemed to get dropped with a bunch of stuff and...
Content_zh: 因為不然我們就會把它往後推，結果 Camden 好像會被丟一堆東西，然後……

Content: Petty: That sounds like the perfect solution to me.
Content_zh: Petty：這聽起來是完美的解決方法。

Content: Rice: Well, and Ray and the animators are just, yeah, going to be looking for stuff.
Content_zh: Rice：而且 Ray 跟動畫師們也會在等東西做。

Content: Crook: Effects artist...
Content_zh: Crook：特效美術……

Content: Schafer: Push him a little farther until he gets grumpy..
Content_zh: Schafer：再多逼他一點，逼到他開始暴躁……

Content: Petty: Oh the effects artist we're going to hire.
Content_zh: Petty：喔，我們要聘的那位特效美術。

Content: Schafer: Still trying to make December.
Content_zh: Schafer：還在努力拚十二月。

Content: I mean a lot of it's hard for people to commit to something being done on a certain date if a lot of things are unknown.
Content_zh: 很多事情還未知時，要人承諾某個日期會完成，本來就很難。

Content: Like we don't know what the final act is going to look like.
Content_zh: 像我們還不知道最後一幕會長什麼樣子。

Content: And like I can see it, but if they don't know exactly what all the assets are going to be, it's hard for them to say it will be done by December.
Content_zh: 我自己看得見，但如果他們不知道所有資產到底會有哪些，就很難說十二月能完成。

Content: But I still feel good about it.
Content_zh: 但我還是覺得有信心。

Content: Schafer: I, I think I found it and brought it in and left it as a gift for my producers.
Content_zh: Schafer：我想我找到了它，帶進來留給我的製作人當禮物。

Content: Just to give them something I could never actually give them in real life.
Content_zh: 給他們一個我現實生活中永遠沒辦法真正給的東西。

Content: I mean, if we're not done in December, we'd definitely be done in January, that's the thing.
Content_zh: 我是說，如果十二月做不完，一月一定會完成，重點就是這個。

Content: So it just seems like so close, like why miss it.
Content_zh: 所以感覺就差這麼一點，為什麼要錯過？

Content: Schafer: I finally finished the Act Two girl dialogue, so the spaceship dialogue is done,
Content_zh: Schafer：我終於寫完第二幕女孩線的對話，所以太空船對話完成了，

Content: which is exciting for me personally, thank you, Anna.
Content_zh: 這對我個人來說很振奮，謝謝你，Anna。

Content: Yeah, it's between us.
Content_zh: 對，這是我們之間的事。

Content: It's really important.
Content_zh: 這很重要。

Content: So now I'm just writing, I just to have to write the finale, which looks like this.
Content_zh: 所以我現在就繼續寫，只剩結局要寫，長這樣。

Content: Had a lot of spoilers right there.
Content_zh: 那裡有很多劇透。

Content: Don't film that part.
Content_zh: 別拍那部分。

Content: Schafer: I got to finish.
Content_zh: Schafer：我得把它寫完。

Content: I'm so close to being done with the dialogue for the game, which would be a huge milestone in my life,
Content_zh: 我離寫完這款遊戲的對話已經很近了，那會是我人生中的巨大里程碑，

Content: 'cause all I can think about is dialogue for this game.
Content_zh: 因為我腦中全都是這款遊戲的對話。

Content: There's a lot of dialogue in this game.
Content_zh: 這遊戲有很多對話。

Content: Offscreen Voice: So why are you standing up?
Content_zh: 畫外音：所以你為什麼站著？

Content: Schafer: I'm standing up 'cause I had my gall bladder out.
Content_zh: Schafer：我站著是因為我切了膽囊。

Content: I'm trying to act cool so people don't know that I had it out, but I don't like to sit down,
Content_zh: 我想裝得很酷，讓大家不知道我切了膽囊，但我不太想坐下，

Content: 'cause if I fold the part where they cut me open and take it out, it kind of hurts.
Content_zh: 因為如果彎到他們切開把它拿出來的那個地方，會有點痛。

Content: But that was on Friday.
Content_zh: 不過那是星期五的事。

Content: And now it's Monday, I'm fine.
Content_zh: 現在星期一了，我沒事。

Content: It's no big deal, gall bladders are stupid, overrated.
Content_zh: 沒什麼大不了。膽囊很笨，被高估了。

Content: Offscreen Voice: Is that because of the juice diet?
Content_zh: 畫外音：是因為果汁飲食嗎？

Content: Schafer: No one knows if it was because of the juice diet or not.
Content_zh: Schafer：沒人知道是不是因為果汁飲食。

Content: Like some people, the doctor said it's just genetic and you're just prone to having gall bladders, gall bladder stones, gallstones.
Content_zh: 醫生說有些人就是遺傳，天生容易有膽囊……膽囊結石，膽結石。

Content: But my wife is sure it's the juice diet.
Content_zh: 但我太太很確定是果汁飲食害的。

Content: Offscreen Voice: So no more juice?
Content_zh: 畫外音：所以不喝果汁了？

Content: Schafer: I don't know.
Content_zh: Schafer：我不知道。

Content: This has been a long project.
Content_zh: 這個專案真的很長。

Content: I've been through many bodily changes through this project.
Content_zh: 這個專案期間，我的身體經歷了很多變化。

Content: Stress. It's probably just stress, games gonna kill me.
Content_zh: 壓力。大概只是壓力，做遊戲會害死我。

Content: Oh, my tush is going to hurt.
Content_zh: 喔，我屁股要痛了。

Content: I'm going to sit. I have to sit down.
Content_zh: 我要坐。我得坐下。

Content: That pen's a goner, 'cause I can't go down there and get it.
Content_zh: 那支筆沒救了，因為我沒辦法彎下去撿。

Content: [Music] Schafer: Last week we went to PAX, that was awesome.
Content_zh: [音樂] Schafer：上週我們去了 PAX，很棒。

Content: We didn't do a bunch of Broken Age stuff 'cause Broken Age is all stealth, Since we're showing the other games.
Content_zh: 我們沒有做太多《Broken Age》的活動，因為我們在展示其他遊戲時，《Broken Age》都還是保密狀態。

Content: Rice: A lot of people asked about it, though.
Content_zh: Rice：但很多人都問起這件事。

Content: Schafer: A lot of people asked about it, a lot of people said they loved Part One.
Content_zh: Schafer：很多人問起它，也有很多人說他們很喜歡第一幕。

Content: Rice: Exactly. Schafer: And that was nice.
Content_zh: Rice：沒錯。Schafer：那感覺很好。

Content: Rice: I actually had a lot of people say don't make it harder.
Content_zh: Rice：其實很多人跟我說，不要把它變難。

Content: That was like the comment and feedback I got.
Content_zh: 那就是我收到的留言和回饋。

Content: Schafer: Shit. Kipnis: Well, it's too late now.
Content_zh: Schafer：糟了。Kipnis：嗯，現在太晚了。

Content: Rice: Yeah, they're all talking about how they like to play with their kids and how it's a too great experience.
Content_zh: Rice：對，他們都說很喜歡跟孩子一起玩，覺得那是很棒的體驗。

Content: So, something to keep in mind.
Content_zh: 所以，這點要記在心裡。

Content: Petty: It's a generational game.
Content_zh: Petty：這是跨世代遊戲。

Content: [Garbled noises] Rice: Creepy.
Content_zh: [含糊雜音] Rice：好可怕。
